title: Control of oxidizing biocide reagents to mitigate biofouling
abstract: The present disclosure provides methods of monitoring and controlling biocides in an aqueous medium, methods of manufacturing the compositions, and various oxidizing biocide compositions suitable for use in aqueous medium treatment processes. Methods include adding a first oxidizing biocide at a first injection point upstream of a reverse osmosis (RO) membrane inlet, adding a second oxidizing biocide at a second injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet, obtaining a first sample of the aqueous medium from a first location downstream of the first and second injection points and upstream of the RO membrane inlet, and obtaining a second sample of the aqueous medium from a second location downstream of the first location but immediately upstream of the RO membrane. Methods may also include measuring free residual oxidant concentration, total residual oxidant concentration, and oxidation-reduction potential of the first and second samples and adjusting biocide addition based on the measurements.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A method of monitoring and controlling biocides in an aqueous medium, comprising: adding a first oxidizing biocide at a first injection point upstream of a reverse osmosis (RO) membrane inlet; adding a second oxidizing biocide at a second injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet; obtaining a first sample of the aqueous medium from a first location downstream of the first and second injection points and upstream of the RO membrane inlet; obtaining a second sample of the aqueous medium from a second location downstream of the first location but upstream of the RO membrane inlet; measuring a free residual oxidant concentration of the first sample and the second sample using a reagent comprising N, N-diethyl-p- phenylenediamine (DPD); and adjusting addition of the first and/or second oxidizing biocide so that the free residual oxidant concentration in the second sample is less than about 0.1 mg/L as Ch and the free residual oxidant concentration in the first sample is higher than about 0.1 mg/L as Ch . 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the reagent further comprises a buffering agent, a chelating agent, or any combination thereof. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are selected from the group consisting of chlorosulfamate, bromosulfamate, iodosulfamate, urea, a stabilized chlorine or a stabilized 

bromine stabilized by ammonia, ethanolamine, or organic sulfamate, and any combination thereof. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the first oxidizing biocide is the stabilized bromine. 5. The method of claim 3 or claim 4, wherein the second oxidizing biocide is the stabilized chlorine. 6. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising adding a third biocide at a third injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the third biocide is selected from the group consisting of dibromonitrilopropionamide (DBNPA), an isothiazolinone (CMIT/MIT) chlorosulfamate, bromosulfamate, iodosulfamate, a stabilized chlorine or stabilized bromine stabilized by ammonia, urea, ethanolamine, or organic sulfamate, and any combination thereof. 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first oxidizing biocide is added simultaneously with the second oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the second oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second oxidizing biocide is added simultaneously with the first oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the first oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 10. The method of claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the third biocide is added simultaneously with the first and/or second oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the first and/or second oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 11. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are added simultaneously or intermittently and the first and second injection points are at substantially the same location. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are added simultaneously or intermittently and the first injection point is further from the RO membrane than the second injection point or wherein the second injection point is further from the RO membrane than the first injection point. 13. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first oxidizing biocide comprises from about 1 mol % to about 50 mol % of a total amount of biocide added. 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the second oxidizing biocide comprises from about 50 mol % to about 99 mol % of a total amount of biocide added. 15. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the free residual oxidant concentration in the second sample is less than about 0.05 mg/L as Ch . 16. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the aqueous medium comprises wastewater. 17. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first injection point and/or the second injection point is before a cartridge filter, a feeding tank, an ion-exchange unit, an activated carbon filtration unit, a micro/ultrafiltration unit, and/or a sand filtration unit. 18. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising consuming the first oxidizing biocide before it reaches the RO membrane inlet. 19. A method of monitoring and controlling biocides in an aqueous medium, comprising: adding a first oxidizing biocide at a first injection point upstream of a RO membrane inlet; adding a second oxidizing biocide at a second injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet; obtaining a first sample of the aqueous medium from a first location downstream of the first and second injection points and upstream of the RO membrane inlet; obtaining a second sample of the aqueous medium from a second location downstream of the first location and upstream of the RO membrane inlet; measuring a total residual oxidant concentration of the first sample using a reagent comprising DPD; measuring a total residual oxidant concentration of the second sample using the reagent comprising DPD; and adjusting biocide addition based on a difference between the measured total residual oxidant concentration at the second location and the measured total residual oxidant concentration at the first location. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising: adjusting an amount of the first and/or second oxidizing biocide being added so that the total residual oxidant concentration of the second sample is lower than or equal to 98% of the total residual oxidant concentration of the first sample. 21 . The method of claim 19, further comprising: reducing an amount of the first and/or second oxidizing biocide being added if the total residual oxidant concentration is greater than 100 ppm. 22. The method of any one of claims 19 to 21 , wherein the reagent further comprises a buffering agent, a chelating agent, or any combination thereof. 23. The method of any one of claims 19 to 22, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are selected from the group consisting of chlorosulfamate, bromosulfamate, iodosulfamate, a stabilized chlorine or a stabilized bromine stabilized by ammonia, urea, ethanolamine, or organic sulfamate, and any combination thereof. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the first oxidizing biocide is the stabilized bromine. 25. The method of claim 23 or claim 24, wherein the second oxidizing biocide is the stabilized chlorine. 26. The method of any one of claims 19 to 25, further comprising adding a third biocide at a third injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the third biocide is selected from the group consisting of dibromo nitrilopropionamide (DBNPA), chlorosulfamate, bromosulfamate, iodosulfamate, a stabilized chlorine or a stabilized bromine stabilized by ammonia, urea, ethanolamine, or organic sulfamate, and any combination thereof. 28. The method of any one of claims 19 to 27, wherein the first oxidizing biocide is added simultaneously with the second oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the second oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 29. The method of any one of claims 19 to 28, wherein the second oxidizing biocide is added simultaneously with the first oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the first oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 30. The method of claim 26 or claim 27, wherein the third biocide is added simultaneously with the first and/or second oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the first and/or second oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 31 . The method of any one of claims 19 to 30, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are added simultaneously or intermittently and the first and second injection points are at substantially the same location. 32. The method of any one of claims 19 to 30, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are added simultaneously or intermittently and the first injection point is further from the RO membrane than the second injection point or wherein the second injection point is further from the RO membrane than the first injection point. 33. The method of any one of claims 19 to 32, wherein the first biocide comprises from about 1 mol % to about 50 mol % of a total amount of biocide added. 34. The method of any one of claims 19 to 33, wherein the second biocide comprises from about 50 mol % to about 99 mol % of a total amount of biocide added. 35. The method of any one of claims 19 to 34, wherein the total residual oxidant concentration is greater than about 100 ppm. 36. The method of any one of claims 19 to 35, wherein the aqueous medium comprises wastewater. 37. The method of any one of claims 19 to 36, wherein the first injection point and/or the second injection point is before a cartridge filter, a feeding tank, an ion-exchange unit, an activated carbon filtration unit, a micro/ultrafiltration unit, and/or a sand filtration unit. 38. The method of any one of claims 19 to 37, further comprising consuming the first oxidizing biocide before it reaches the RO membrane inlet. 39. A method of monitoring and controlling biocides in wastewater comprising: adding a first oxidizing biocide at a first injection point upstream of a RO membrane inlet; adding a second oxidizing biocide at a second injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet; measuring a first oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) at a first location downstream of the first and second injection points but upstream of the RO membrane inlet; and adjusting an amount of the first and/or second biocide being added to maintain the first ORP higher than about 300 mV. 40. The method of claim 39, further comprising measuring a second ORP at a second location, which is downstream of the first location but upstream of the RO membrane inlet; calculating a difference in the ORP between the second and first locations; and adjusting an amount of the first and/or second oxidizing biocide being added to maintain the first ORP at least about 30 mV higher than the second ORP. 41. The method of claim 39 or claim 40, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are selected from the group consisting of chlorosulfamate, bromosulfamate, iodosulfamate, a stabilized chlorine or a stabilized bromine stabilized by ammonia, urea, ethanolamine, or organic sulfamate, and any combination thereof. 42. The method of claim 41 , wherein the first oxidizing biocide is the stabilized bromine. 43. The method of claim 41 or claim 42, wherein the second oxidizing biocide is the stabilized chlorine. 44. The method of any one of claims 39 to 43, further comprising adding a third biocide at a third injection point upstream of the RO membrane inlet. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the third biocide is selected from the group consisting of dibromonitrilopropionamide, isothiazolinone, chlorosulfamate, bromosulfamate, iodosulfamate, a stabilized chlorine or a stabilized bromine stabilized by ammonia, urea, ethanolamine, or organic sulfamate, and any combination thereof. 46. The method of any one of claims 39 to 45, wherein the first oxidizing biocide is added simultaneously with the second oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the second oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 47. The method of any one of claims 39 to 46, wherein the second oxidizing biocide is added simultaneously with the first oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the first oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 48. The method of claim 44 or claim 45, wherein the third biocide is added simultaneously with the first and/or second oxidizing biocide, sequentially with the first and/or second oxidizing biocide, continuously, and/or intermittently. 49. The method of any one of claims 39 to 48, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are added simultaneously or intermittently and the first and second injection points are at substantially the same location. 50. The method of any one of claims 39 to 48, wherein the first and second oxidizing biocides are added simultaneously or intermittently and the first injection point is further from the RO membrane than the second injection point or wherein the second injection point is further from the RO membrane than the first injection point. 51. The method of any one of claims 39 to 50, wherein the first biocide comprises from about 1 mol % to about 50 mol % of a total amount of biocide added. 52. The method of any one of claims 39 to 51 , wherein the second biocide comprises from about 50 mol % to about 99 mol % of a total amount of biocide added. 53. The method of any one of claims 39 to 52, wherein the aqueous medium comprises wastewater. 54. The method of any one of claims 39 to 53, wherein the first injection point and/or the second injection point is before a cartridge filter, a feeding tank, an ion-exchange unit, an activated carbon filtration unit, a micro/ultrafiltration unit, and/or a sand filtration unit. 56. The method of any one of claims 39 to 55, further comprising consuming the first oxidizing biocide before it reaches the RO membrane inlet.
